task_completion.description: OpenICE application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE GUI is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The three required simulated devices are created in OpenICE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Instances of 'Multiparameter Monitor', 'Pulse Oximeter', and 'Infusion Pump' are visible as active or simulated devices within the OpenICE interface.

task_completion.description: The target CSV file is created and opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'device_identity_map.csv' is open in a text editor or spreadsheet application, with the path indicating it is on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/).

task_completion.description: The CSV file contains the exact required header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The first line or row of the CSV file is exactly 'DeviceType,UUID'.

task_completion.description: The CSV file contains rows for each of the three required devices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CSV file has exactly three data rows below the header, listing 'Multiparameter Monitor', 'Pulse Oximeter', and 'Infusion Pump'.

task_completion.description: Valid UUIDs are recorded for each device in the CSV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Next to each device name in the CSV, there is a 36-character UUID following the standard 8-4-4-4-12 hexadecimal format (e.g., 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenICE GUI to obtain the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the OpenICE interface to view device details and UUIDs, rather than just using terminal commands to spoof the file.

integrity.description: The UUIDs appear to be genuine and not placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UUIDs in the CSV look like randomly generated hexadecimal strings matching the OpenICE device instances, not sequential numbers or obvious placeholders like '1111-2222...'.

integrity.description: The CSV file is saved in the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file manager, terminal, or text editor title bar confirms the file is located at /home/ga/Desktop/device_identity_map.csv.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating exactly three simulated devices in OpenICE: 'Multiparameter Monitor', 'Pulse Oximeter', and 'Infusion Pump'. The target file must be at /home/ga/Desktop/device_identity_map.csv. The CSV must have the exact header 'DeviceType,UUID'. The UUIDs must follow the standard 36-character v4 format (8-4-4-4-12 hexadecimal digits separated by hyphens).
